decision: ADR-0040 compliant replacement: deterministic rail/vertex geometry plus generated/source visible pixels

native_scale_audit.reason: visible generated/source pixels are spatially downscaled before the final 1x runtime frames are written
native_scale_audit.next_acceptance_step: regenerate the approved material texel density and post subjects at their required native footprints, remove spatial resizing from the accepted bake, and add a native-size family gate

topology.automatic_post_rule: draw at a geometric fence vertex iff unique incident segment degree is exactly 1
topology.authored_post_rule: draw at any explicitly stored in-bounds logical vertex; explicit material overrides an automatic post at the same vertex
topology.authored_post_wire: EditorBoard.fencePosts / board-code fp, keyed by canonical vx,vy with inclusive bounds 0..cols and 0..rows
topology.boundary_behavior: north/west edges resolve through off-board phantom owners and share the same vertex-degree graph

materials.normalization: 64x64 source is spatially resized to 48x48, then colors are reduced and projected through alpha-only wood rail geometry; calibration bridge only under ADR-0076
1024x1024 source is spatially resized to 48x48, then colors are reduced and projected through alpha-only low-wall geometry; calibration bridge only under ADR-0076

posts.gate: image_generation_call in the current Codex task rollout
posts.prompt: Create exactly one stout oak fence terminal post for an isometric pixel-art chess tactics game: more substantial than an ordinary picket, with a small dark forged-iron band and restrained pyramidal wooden cap. One isolated vertical square timber post only, no rails or scenery. Crisp low-resolution pixel art, orthographic isometric three-quarter view matching a 2:1 board diamond, readable at about 24 pixels tall after downscaling. Soft top-left board lighting, warm muted oak, flat solid #00ff00 chroma background, no shadow, text, or watermark.

posts.gate: image_generation_call in the current Codex task rollout
posts.prompt: Create exactly one squat masonry terminal pier for the open end of a low stone fence in an isometric pixel-art chess tactics game, visibly more substantial than the adjoining low wall and topped by a clean beveled capstone. One isolated square stone pier only, no attached wall, rubble, or scenery. Crisp low-resolution pixel art, orthographic isometric three-quarter view matching a 2:1 board diamond, readable at about 22 pixels tall after downscaling. Soft top-left board lighting, cool weathered gray stone, flat solid #00ff00 chroma background, no shadow, text, or watermark.

chroma_removal.runtime_alpha: bake thresholds final 96x180 calibration-bridge frames to hard 0/255 alpha; this is not native-scale acceptance
